paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Pheidole dentata, a native ant of the south-eastern U.S., isn't immortal. But scientists have found that it doesn't seem to show any signs of aging. Old worker ants can do everything just as well as the youngsters, and their brains appear just as sharp. 'We get a picture that these ants really don't decline,' says Ysabel Giraldo, who studied the ants for her doctoral thesis at Boston University.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Such age-defying feats are rare in the animal kingdom. Naked mole rats can live for almost 30 years and stay fit for nearly their entire lives. They can still reproduce even when old, and they never get cancer. But the vast majority of animals deteriorate with age just like people do. Like the naked mole rat, ants are social creatures that usually live in highly organised colonies. 'It's this social complexity that makes P. dentata useful for studying aging in people,' says Giraldo, now at the California Institute of Technology. Humans are also highly social, a trait that has been connected to healthier aging. By contrast, most animal studies of aging use mice, worms or fruit flies, which all lead much more isolated lives.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In the lab, P. dentata worker ants typically live for around 140 days. Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges: 20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days. Unlike all previous studies, which only estimated how old the ants were, her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults, so she knew their exact ages. Then she put them through a range of tests.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Giraldo watched how well the ants took care of the young of the colony, recording how often each ant attended to, carried and fed them. She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food. She tested how ants responded to light and also measured how active they were by counting how often ants in a small dish walked across a line. And she experimented with how ants react to live prey: a tethered fruit fly. Giraldo expected the older ants to perform poorly in all these tasks. But the elderly insects were all good caretakers and trail-followers — the 95-day-old ants could track the scent even longer than their younger counterparts. They all responded to light well, and the older ants were more active. And when it came to reacting to prey, the older ants attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did, flaring their mandibles or pulling at the fly's legs.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death. She saw no major differences with age, nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells, showing that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions. Ants and other insects have structures in their brains called mushroom bodies, which are important for processing information, learning and memory. She also wanted to see if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures—regions where neurons come together. Again, the answer was no. What was more, the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging. In humans, for example, a decrease in serotonin has been linked to Alzheimer's disease.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: 'This is the first time anyone has looked at both behavioral and neural changes in these ants so thoroughly,' says Giraldo, who recently published the findings in the Proceedings of the Royal Society B. Scientists have looked at some similar aspects in bees, but the results of recent bee studies were mixed—some studies showed age-related declines, which biologists call senescence, and others didn't. 'For now, the study raises more questions than it answers,' Giraldo says, 'including how P. dentata stays in such good shape.'

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Also, if the ants don't deteriorate with age, why do they die at all? Out in the wild, the ants probably don't live for a full 140 days thanks to predators, disease and just being in an environment that's much harsher than the comforts of the lab. 'The lucky ants that do live into old age may suffer a steep decline just before dying,' Giraldo says, but she can't say for sure because her study wasn't designed to follow an ant's final moments.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: 'It will be important to extend these findings to other species of social insects,' says Gene E. Robinson, an entomologist at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. This ant might be unique, or it might represent a broader pattern among other social bugs with possible clues to the science of aging in larger animals. Either way, it seems that for these ants, age really doesn't matter.

groups.title: Ysabel Giraldo's research
groups.desc:
Questions 1-8
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.
groups.collect: Focused on a total of $755$different age groups of ants, analysing Behaviour: • how well ants looked after their $756$ • their ability to locate $757$using a scent trail • the effect that $758$had on them • how $759$they attacked prey Brains: • comparison between age and the $760$of dying cells in the brains of ants • condition of synaptic complexes (areas in which $761$meet) in the brain's 'mushroom bodies' • level of two $762$ in the brain associated with ageing

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Focused on a total of 【 】different age groups of ants”对应文章中的“Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges”。“Focused on” 和 “total of” 在原文中直接对应，而“different age groups”在原文中对应的是“age ranges”。因此，答案是“four/4”。

central_sentences.sentence: Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges: 20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days.
central_sentences.translation: Giraldo 关注了四个年龄段的蚂蚁：20至22天、45至47天、95至97天和120至122天。

target_sen.target_sen: Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges: 20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days.
Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges: 20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days.

questions.analysis: 答案是“young”，因为题干中的“looked after”在原文中对应的是“took care of”，“their”对应的是“the”，而“young”是被照顾的对象。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以确定答案句中的“young”就是题干中所缺的词。

central_sentences.sentence: Giraldo watched how well the ants took care of the young of the colony, recording how often each ant attended to, carried and fed them.
central_sentences.translation: Giraldo观察蚂蚁照顾蚁群幼虫的情况，记录每只蚂蚁照顾、搬运和喂养幼虫的频率。

target_sen.target_sen: Giraldo watched how well the ants took care of the young of the colony, recording how often each ant attended to, carried and fed them.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "locate" 对应原文中的 "mark"，"scent trail" 对应原文中的 "telltale scent" 和 "trail"。原文句子明确指出蚂蚁使用气味路径来标记食物，因此答案是 "food"。

central_sentences.sentence: She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food.
central_sentences.translation: 她比较了20天龄和95天龄的蚂蚁在跟随昆虫通常留下的标记食物踪迹的气味方面的表现。

target_sen.target_sen: She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food.
She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "the effect that 【 】had on them" 对应文章中的 "how ants responded to light"。这里的 "responded to" 和 "effect" 是同义表达，而 "light" 则是需要填入的答案。通过找到题干中的线索词 "effect" 和 "had on them"，并在文章中找到对应的同义替换词 "responded to"，我们可以确定答案是 "light"。

central_sentences.sentence: She tested how ants responded to light and also measured how active they were by counting how often ants in a small dish walked across a line.
central_sentences.translation: 她测试了蚂蚁对光的反应，并通过计算蚂蚁在小碟中穿过一条线的频率来测量它们的活跃程度。

target_sen.target_sen: She tested how ants responded to light and also measured how active they were by counting how often ants in a small dish walked across a line.
They all responded to light well, and the older ants were more active.

questions.analysis: 题干问的是“how they attacked prey”，即“它们如何攻击猎物”。在原文中，明确提到“attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did”，其中“aggressively”描述了攻击的方式。因此，答案是“aggressively”，因为它直接回答了题干中的“how”这个问题，解释了蚂蚁攻击猎物的方式。

central_sentences.sentence: And when it came to reacting to prey, the older ants attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did, flaring their mandibles or pulling at the fly's legs.
central_sentences.translation: 当涉及到对猎物的反应时，老蚂蚁攻击可怜的果蝇时就像年轻蚂蚁一样具有攻击性，它们张开下颚或拉扯果蝇的腿。

target_sen.target_sen: And when it came to reacting to prey, the older ants attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did, flaring their mandibles or pulling at the fly's legs.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "location"。题干要求比较年龄和濒死细胞的某个特征。原文中明确提到 "nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells"，即濒死细胞的位置没有差异。因此，正确答案是 "location"。

central_sentences.sentence: She saw no major differences with age, nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells, showing that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions.
central_sentences.translation: 她没有发现随年龄变化有任何重大差异，濒死细胞的位置也没有任何差异，表明年龄似乎不会影响特定的脑功能。

target_sen.target_sen: Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.
Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.

target_sen.target_sen: Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.
Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“synaptic complexes”和“mushroom bodies”在原文中直接对应。题干中的“areas in which 【 】meet”在原文中对应“regions where neurons come together”。由此可见，答案是“neurons”，因为它是题干中【 】应填入的词。

central_sentences.sentence: She also wanted to see if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures—regions where neurons come together.
central_sentences.translation: 她还想看看衰老是否会影响这些结构中突触复合体的密度——神经元聚集的区域。

target_sen.target_sen: She also wanted to see if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures—regions where neurons come together.
Ants and other insects have structures in their brains called mushroom bodies, which are important for processing information, learning and memory.
She also wanted to see if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures—regions where neurons come together.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "level of two 【 】 in the brain associated with ageing" 需要填入一个名词，表示与衰老相关的两种大脑物质的水平。通过定位题干中的线索词 "level"、"brain" 和 "associated with ageing"，我们在原文中找到了对应的句子。原文中提到 "levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging"，这里的 "brain chemicals" 是泛指的名词，符合题干要求。因此，正确答案是 "chemicals"。

central_sentences.sentence: What was more, the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging.
central_sentences.translation: 更重要的是，老年蚂蚁的血清素或多巴胺水平没有出现任何下降——这些脑部化学物质的减少通常与衰老有关。

target_sen.target_sen: What was more, the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging.
Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.
She saw no major differences with age, nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells, showing that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions.
Ants and other insects have structures in their brains called mushroom bodies, which are important for processing information, learning and memory.
What was more, the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging.

groups.desc:
Questions 9-13

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Pheidole dentata ants are the only known animals which remain active for almost their whole lives.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“only known animals”表示Pheidole dentata蚂蚁是唯一已知的能在几乎整个生命周期中保持活跃的动物。然而，原文提到“裸鼹鼠可以活近30年，并且几乎在整个生命周期中都保持健康”，这表明Pheidole dentata蚂蚁并不是唯一一种具有这种特征的动物。因此，题干中的陈述与文章内容相矛盾，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Such age-defying feats are rare in the animal kingdom. Naked mole rats can live for almost 30 years and stay fit for nearly their entire lives.
central_sentences.translation: 这种抗衰老的特征在动物界是罕见的。裸鼹鼠可以活近30年，并且在几乎整个生命周期中保持健康。

target_sen.target_sen: Such age-defying feats are rare in the animal kingdom.
Naked mole rats can live for almost 30 years and stay fit for nearly their entire lives.
Naked mole rats can live for almost 30 years and stay fit for nearly their entire lives.

questions.title: Ysabel Giraldo was the first person to study Pheidole dentata ants using precise data about the insects' ages.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "first person" 对应原文中的 "Unlike all previous studies"，表明 Ysabel Giraldo 是第一个使用这种方法的人。题干中的 "precise data about the insects' ages" 对应原文中的 "knew their exact ages"，表明她使用了精确的年龄数据。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Unlike all previous studies, which only estimated how old the ants were, her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults, so she knew their exact ages.
central_sentences.translation: 与所有先前的研究不同，那些研究只是估算蚂蚁的年龄，而她的工作从蚁蛹变成成虫开始就跟踪记录，因此她知道它们的确切年龄。

target_sen.target_sen: Unlike all previous studies, which only estimated how old the ants were, her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults, so she knew their exact ages.
Unlike all previous studies, which only estimated how old the ants were, her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults, so she knew their exact ages.

questions.title: The ants in Giraldo's experiments behaved as she had predicted that they would.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "predicted" 在原文中对应 "expected"，"behaved" 对应 "performed"。原文明确指出 Giraldo 预期老蚂蚁表现会很差，但实际结果却显示老蚂蚁表现得很好，这与她的预测相反。因此，题干陈述的信息是错误的，答案为 "FALSE"。

central_sentences.sentence: Giraldo expected the older ants to perform poorly in all these tasks. But the elderly insects were all good caretakers and trail-followers — the 95-day-old ants could track the scent even longer than their younger counterparts.
central_sentences.translation: Giraldo预期年老的蚂蚁在所有这些任务中表现不佳。但是这些年老的昆虫都是很好的照料者和踪迹跟随者——95天大的蚂蚁甚至能比它们年轻的同类追踪气味更长时间。

target_sen.target_sen: Giraldo expected the older ants to perform poorly in all these tasks.
Giraldo expected the older ants to perform poorly in all these tasks.

questions.title: The recent studies of bees used different methods of measuring age-related decline.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "recent studies of bees" 在原文中找到了对应的 "recent bee studies"，但题干中的 "different methods" 在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词。原文只提到研究结果是混合的，但没有提到使用了不同的方法。因此，题干中的信息在原文中找不到，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Scientists have looked at some similar aspects in bees, but the results of recent bee studies were mixed—some studies showed age-related declines, which biologists call senescence, and others didn't.
central_sentences.translation: 科学家们研究了蜜蜂的一些类似方面，但最近的蜜蜂研究结果是混合的——有些研究显示了与年龄相关的衰退（生物学家称之为衰老），而有些则没有。

target_sen.target_sen: Scientists have looked at some similar aspects in bees, but the results of recent bee studies were mixed—some studies showed age-related declines, which biologists call senescence, and others didn't.

questions.title: Pheidole dentata ants kept in laboratory conditions tend to live longer lives.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“laboratory conditions”在原文中对应“in the lab”，“live longer lives”对应“live for around 140 days”和“don't live for a full 140 days”。通过对比可以看出，在实验室条件下，蚂蚁的寿命更长。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: In the lab, P. dentata worker ants typically live for around 140 days.
central_sentences.translation: 在实验室里，P. dentata工蚁通常能活约140天。

central_sentences.sentence: Out in the wild, the ants probably don't live for a full 140 days thanks to predators, disease and just being in an environment that's much harsher than the comforts of the lab.
central_sentences.translation: 在野外，由于捕食者、疾病以及环境比实验室恶劣得多，这些蚂蚁可能活不到完整的140天。

target_sen.target_sen: In the lab, P. dentata worker ants typically live for around 140 days.
In the lab, P. dentata worker ants typically live for around 140 days.

content.title: Why zoos are good
content.summary: {'A': '动物园或野生动物公园中的许多动物可以拥有与野外相当或更高的生活质量，因为它们可以获得多样化和高质量的饮食、得到必要的补充物，并接受治疗，同时它们还能在安全的环境中生活，免受同类的欺凌和社交排斥，不会受到捕食者的威胁或压力，也不会受到寄生虫或受伤的刺激和疼痛。与野生动物相比，平均圈养动物的预期寿命更长，不会死于干旱、饥饿或被捕食者咬死。好的动物园可以发挥多个重要作用。', 'B': '动物园在保护物种方面发挥了重要作用，通过在囚禁环境中保护和繁殖物种，可以防止野外物种数量的崩溃和灭绝，同时也为野外物种提供了补充种群，使得更多物种得以幸存和重新引入野外。', 'C': '动物园也提供教育。许多城市的儿童和成年人可能永远不会见到野生动物，除了狐狸或鸽子。虽然电视纪录片越来越详细和令人印象深刻，许多自然历史标本也在博物馆展出，但与亲眼看到活生生的动物、听到它们的声音、闻到它们的气味、观察它们的行为并有时间去吸收细节相比，真的没有什么可以比拟的。这一点将使许多人更好地理解和看待野生动物，保护努力以及他们如何做出贡献，并希望能让他们更加珍惜野生动物。', 'D': '这段话主要讲了动物园通过标志、讲座和演示等方式向游客传达信息，以及动物园在教育保护工作者和动物饲养员方面的工作，以改善全球的动物保护条件和重新引入工作。\n简短总结：动物园通过教育和合作工作来改善动物保护和重新引入工作。', 'E': '动物园在研究中也起着关键作用，通过在动物园进行研究，可以更好地了解关键物种的生活、行为和反应，从而对野生种群产生实质性的影响，帮助管理野生种群，减少人与动物的冲突，并为应对栖息地破坏等问题提供知识基础。', 'F': '总结：这段话主要讲了动物园对于保护物种、教育公众和提高环境意识的重要性，认为考虑到环境面临的威胁，动物园对于许多物种的长期生存至关重要，没有它们，世界将变得更加贫瘠。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In my view, it is perfectly possible for many species of animals living in zoos or wildlife parks to have a quality of life as high as, or higher than, in the wild. Animals in good zoos get a varied and high-quality diet with all the supplements required, and any illnesses they might have will be treated. Their movement might be somewhat restricted, but they have a safe environment in which to live, and they are spared bullying and social ostracism by others of their kind. They do not suffer from the threat or stress of predators, or the irritation and pain of parasites or injuries. The average captive animal will have a greater life expectancy compared with its wild counterpart, and will not die of drought, starvation or in the jaws of a predator. A lot of very nasty things happen to truly 'wild' animals that simply don't happen in good zoos, and to view a life that is 'free' as one that is automatically 'good' is, I think, an error. Furthermore, zoos serve several key purposes.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Firstly, zoos aid conservation. Colossal numbers of species are becoming extinct across the world, and many more are increasingly threatened and therefore risk extinction. Moreover, some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected, or were simply discovered very late in the day. A species protected in captivity can be bred up to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild. A good number of species only exist in captivity, with many of these living in zoos. Still more only exist in the wild because they have been reintroduced from zoos, or have wild populations that have been boosted by captive-bred animals. Without these efforts there would be fewer species alive today. Although reintroduction successes are few and far between, the numbers are increasing and the very fact that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding proves the value of such initiatives.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Zoos also provide education. Many children and adults, especially those in cities, will never see a wild animal beyond a fox or pigeon. While it is true that television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive, and many natural history specimens are on display in museums, there really is nothing to compare with seeing a living creature in the flesh, hearing it, smelling it, watching what it does and having the time to absorb details. That alone will bring a greater understanding and perspective to many, and hopefully give them a greater appreciation for wildlife, conservation efforts and how they can contribute.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: In addition to this, there is also the education that can take place in zoos through signs, talks and presentations which directly communicate information to visitors about the animals they are seeing and their place in the world. This was an area where zoos used to be lacking, but they are now increasingly sophisticated in their communication and outreach work. Many zoos also work directly to educate conservation workers in other countries, or send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills to those working in zoos and reserves, thereby helping to improve conditions and reintroductions all over the world.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Zoos also play a key role in research. If we are to save wild species and restore and repair ecosystems, we need to know about how key species live, act and react. Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected on wild populations. Finding out about, for example, the oestrus cycle of an animal or its breeding rate helps us manage wild populations. Procedures such as capturing and moving at-risk or dangerous individuals are bolstered by knowledge gained in zoos about doses for anaesthetics, and by experience in handling and transporting animals. This can make a real difference to conservation efforts and to the reduction of human-animal conflicts, and can provide a knowledge base for helping with the increasing threats of habitat destruction and other problems.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In conclusion, considering the many ongoing global threats to the environment, it is hard for me to see zoos as anything other than essential to the long-term survival of numerous species. They are vital not just in terms of protecting animals, but as a means of learning about them to aid those still in the wild, as well as educating and informing the general population about these animals and their world so that they can assist or at least accept the need to be more environmentally conscious. Without them, the world would be, and would increasingly become, a much poorer place.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-17
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.

questions.title: a reference to how quickly animal species can die out
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落B，因为段落B中明确提到了物种灭绝的速度。具体来说，句子“some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected”直接对应了题干中的“how quickly animal species can die out”。这些词汇“sudden, dramatic and unexpected”都是“quickly”的同义替换词，而“collapses”则是“die out”的同义替换词。因此，段落B是最符合题干信息的。

central_sentences.sentence: Moreover, some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected, or were simply discovered very late in the day.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，一些物种的灭绝是突然的、戏剧性的和出乎意料的，或者是在很晚的时候才被发现的。

target_sen.target_sen: Moreover, some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected, or were simply discovered very late in the day.
Colossal numbers of species are becoming extinct across the world, and many more are increasingly threatened and therefore risk extinction.
A species protected in captivity can be bred up to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild.

questions.title: reasons why it is preferable to study animals in captivity rather than in the wild
questions.analysis: 这道题的题干要求找出在圈养环境中研究动物比在野外更好的原因。段落E中明确提到在动物园中研究动物的优势是因为有较少的风险和变量，这直接回答了题干的问题。因此，段落E是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected on wild populations.
central_sentences.translation: 能够在风险较小、变量较少的动物园中进行动物研究，意味着可以对野生种群产生实际的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected on wild populations.

questions.title: mention of two ways of learning about animals other than visiting them in zoos
questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“two ways of learning about animals other than visiting them in zoos”在段落C中有明确的对应表达，即“television documentaries”和“natural history specimens on display in museums”。这些表达清楚地指出了除了去动物园以外了解动物的两种方法。因此，段落C是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: While it is true that television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive, and many natural history specimens are on display in museums, there really is nothing to compare with seeing a living creature in the flesh, hearing it, smelling it, watching what it does and having the time to absorb details.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然电视纪录片正变得越来越详细和令人印象深刻，而且许多自然历史标本在博物馆中展出，但实际上没有什么能比得上亲眼看到活生生的生物，听它的声音，闻它的气味，观察它的行为，并有时间吸收细节。

questions.analysis: 从题目、答案和关键词分析得出,该题考查了英文段落中关于动物园工作人员的描述。正确答案B和D是根据文中的'animal keepers'和'conservation workers'这两个关键词来选择的。文中说明动物园工作人员会'send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills to those working in zoos and reserves',以及'educate conservation workers in other countries',与B和D选项相符。

central_sentences.sentence: Many zoos also work directly to educate conservation workers in other countries, or send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills to those working in zoos and reserves, thereby helping to improve conditions and reintroductions all over the world.

A species protected in captivity can be bred up to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild.

Although reintroduction successes are few and far between, the numbers are increasing and the very fact that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding proves the value of such initiatives.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Chelsea Rochman, an ecologist at the University of California, Davis, has been trying to answer a dismal question: Is everything terrible, or are things just very, very bad?

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash. Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Often, Rochman says, scientists will end a paper by speculating about the broader impacts of what they've found. For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out. 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says. 'There wasn't a lot of information.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013. Within each paper, they asked what threats scientists had studied - 366 perceived threats in all - and what they'd actually found.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In 83 percent of cases, the perceived dangers of ocean trash were proven true. In the remaining cases, the working group found the studies had weaknesses in design and content which affected the validity of their conclusions—they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits. The plastic moved from the mussels' stomachs to their bloodstreams, scientists found, and stayed there for weeks—but didn't seem to stress out the shellfish.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: While mussels may be fine eating trash, though, the analysis also gave a clearer picture of the many ways that ocean debris is bothersome.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Within the studies they looked at, most of the proven threats came from plastic debris, rather than other materials like metal or wood. Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris—animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: But a lot of ocean debris is 'microplastic', or pieces smaller than five millimeters. These may be ingredients used in cosmetics and toiletries, fibers shed by synthetic clothing in the wash, or eroded remnants of larger debris. Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris, Rochman's group found little research on the effects of these tiny bits. 'There are a lot of open questions still for microplastic,' Rochman says, though she notes that more papers on the subject have been published since 2013, the cutoff point for the group's analysis.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: There are also, she adds, a lot of open questions about the ways that ocean debris can lead to sea-creature death. Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations. And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what's really in the ocean. None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution—or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: 'We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions,' Rochman says. Usually, scientists don't know exactly how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment until after they've happened. 'We don't ask the right questions early enough,' she says. But if ecologists can understand how the slow-moving effect of ocean trash is damaging ecosystems, they might be able to prevent things from getting worse.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: Asking the right questions can help policy makers, and the public, figure out where to focus their attention. The problems that look or sound most dramatic may not be the best places to start. For example, the name of the 'Great Pacific Garbage Patch'—a collection of marine debris in the northern Pacific Ocean—might conjure up a vast, floating trash island. In reality though, much of the debris is tiny or below the surface; a person could sail through the area without seeing any trash at all. A Dutch group called 'The Ocean Cleanup' is currently working on plans to put mechanical devices in the Pacific Garbage Patch and similar areas to suck up plastic. But a recent paper used simulations to show that strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore would more effectively reduce pollution over the long term.

paragraphs.label: M
paragraphs.text: 'I think clearing up some of these misperceptions is really important,' Rochman says. Among scientists as well as in the media, she says, 'A lot of the images about strandings and entanglement and all of that cause the perception that plastic debris is killing everything in the ocean.' Interrogating the existing scientific literature can help ecologists figure out which problems really need addressing, and which ones they'd be better off—like the mussels—absorbing and ignoring.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-33

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?

TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,

NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Rochman and her colleagues were the first people to research the problem of marine debris.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“first people”在原文中对应的是“plenty of studies”和“a collection of scientists”，这表明在Rochman和她的同事之前已经有很多研究存在。因此，Rochman和她的同事并不是第一批研究海洋垃圾问题的人，这与题干信息相反，所以答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.
central_sentences.translation: 大量研究已经对海洋垃圾的状况发出了警报；在最近发表在《生态学》杂志上的一篇论文中，Rochman和她的同事试图确定这些感知风险中有多少是真实的。

target_sen.target_sen: Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.

Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash.

Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash.

Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.

Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash.

questions.title: The creatures most in danger from ocean trash are certain seabirds.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“most in danger”和“seabirds”。在原文中，虽然提到了“certain seabirds”吃塑料袋并且有灭绝的风险，但并没有明确说明海鸟是“most in danger”的生物。文章中提到的其他生物也受到了海洋垃圾的威胁，但没有进行比较说明哪种生物是最危险的。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有明确提到，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Often, Rochman says，scientists will end a paper by speculating about the broader impacts of what they've found. For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out. 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says. 'There wasn't a lot of information.'
central_sentences.translation: Rochman 说，科学家们通常会在论文结尾推测他们发现的更广泛影响。例如，一项研究可能表明某些海鸟吃塑料袋，并进一步警告整个鸟类种群可能面临灭绝的风险。但事实是，没人真正测试过这些被认为的威胁，'没有太多信息'，Rochman说。

target_sen.target_sen: 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says.
For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out.

questions.title:
The studies Rochman has reviewed have already proved that populations of some
birds will soon become extinct.
questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“proved”（证明）和“extinct”（灭绝）在原文中分别对应“proven”和“dying out”。然而，Rochman明确表示“没有人测试过这些感知到的威胁”，并且“没有很多信息”。这表明题干中的信息是错误的，因为没有证据证明某些鸟类种群即将灭绝。因此，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says. 'There wasn't a lot of information.'
central_sentences.translation: 但事实是，还没有人测试过这些感知到的威胁，"罗奇曼说。"没有太多信息。"

target_sen.target_sen: 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says.
For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out.

questions.title: Rochman analysed papers on the different kinds of danger caused by ocean trash.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“analysed”、“papers”、“danger”和“ocean trash”在原文中分别对应“examined”、“papers”、“impacts”和“marine debris”。原文明确提到Rochman和她的同事们检查了100多篇关于海洋垃圾影响的论文，因此题干信息在文章中是存在的，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013. Within each paper, they asked what threats scientists had studied - 366 perceived threats in all - and what they'd actually found.
central_sentences.translation: Rochman和她的同事审查了2013年之前发表的一百多篇关于海洋垃圾影响的论文。在每篇论文中，他们询问科学家研究了哪些威胁——总共366个被认为的威胁——以及他们实际发现了什么。

syn_ary.synonym: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers

target_sen.target_sen: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013.
Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013.
Within each paper, they asked what threats scientists had studied - 366 perceived threats in all - and what they'd actually found.

locating_words.locating_word: different kinds of danger caused by ocean trash

questions.title:
Most of the research analysed by Rochman and her colleagues was badly
designed.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Most”对应原文中的“83 percent”，而“badly designed”对应“weaknesses in design and content”。原文明确指出，83%的研究结果是被证实的，只有剩下的研究存在设计缺陷。因此，题干中说大多数研究设计不良是不正确的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In 83 percent of cases, the perceived dangers of ocean trash were proven true. In the remaining cases, the working group found the studies had weaknesses in design and content which affected the validity of their conclusions-they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics.
central_sentences.translation: 在83%的案例中，人们认为的海洋垃圾危害被证实是真实的。在剩余的案例中，工作组发现这些研究在设计和内容上存在缺陷，影响了结论的有效性——例如，它们缺乏对照组或使用了错误的统计方法。

target_sen.target_sen: In 83 percent of cases, the perceived dangers of ocean trash were proven true.

In the remaining cases, the working group found the studies had weaknesses in design and content which affected the validity of their conclusions-they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics.

questions.title:
One study examined by Rochman was expecting to find that mussels were harmed
by eating plastic.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“study examined by Rochman”和“mussels were harmed by eating plastic”在原文中分别对应“one well-designed study”和“mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits”。原文提到这项研究“failed to find the effect it was looking for”，即研究预期会发现贻贝因摄入塑料而受到伤害，但最终没有找到这种效果。这表明研究确实预期贻贝会受到伤害，因此答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.
central_sentences.translation: 值得注意的是，Rochman说，只有一项设计良好的研究未能找到它所寻找的效果，这项研究调查了贻贝摄入微小塑料颗粒的情况。

syn_ary.synonym: Rochman says, only one well-designed study

target_sen.target_sen: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.

syn_ary.synonym: an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits

target_sen.target_sen: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.

locating_words.locating_word:
mussels were harmed
by eating plastic

questions.title: Some mussels choose to eat plastic in preference to their natural diet.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Some mussels choose to eat plastic in preference to their natural diet”在文章中没有找到对应的内容。文章提到贻贝摄入了微塑料颗粒，但没有提到它们选择吃塑料而不是自然食物的行为。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits. The plastic moved from the Mussels' stomachs to their bloodstreams, scientists found, and stayed there for weeks - but didn't seem to stress out the shellfish
central_sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，Rochman说，只有一项精心设计的研究未能找到它所寻找的效果，这项研究是关于贻贝摄入微小塑料颗粒的。科学家发现，塑料从贻贝的胃部移动到血液中，并在那里停留了数周 - 但似乎并没有给这些贝类造成压力。

target_sen.target_sen: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.
Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.

groups.title: Findings related to marine debris
groups.desc:
Questions 34-39

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.
groups.collect: Studies of marine debris found the biggest threats were • plastic (not metal or wood) • bits of debris that were $788$(harmful to animals) There was little research into $789$e.g. from synthetic fibres. Drawbacks of the studies examined • most of them focused on individual animals, not entire $790$ • the $791$of plastic used in the lab did not always reflect those in the ocean • there was insufficient information on   -  numbers of animals which could be affected   -  the impact of a reduction in numbers on the $792$of that species   -  the impact on the ecosystem Rochman says more information is needed on the possible impact of future $793$(e.g. involving oil).

questions.analysis: 答案是 "large"。题干中的 "bits of debris" 对应原文中的 "pieces of debris"，"harmful to animals" 对应原文中的 "dangerous to animals" 和 "injuring animals"。原文明确指出，大多数对动物有害的碎片是 "large pieces of debris"。因此，正确答案是 "large"。

central_sentences.sentence: Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris - animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.
central_sentences.translation: 大多数危险也涉及大块碎片 - 例如，动物被垃圾缠住，或者吃下垃圾并严重伤害自己。

target_sen.target_sen: Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris - animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.

syn_ary.synonym: animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves

target_sen.target_sen: Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris - animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.

questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“little research”，在原文中找到了完全相同的表达“little research”。通过阅读上下文，可以确定“these tiny bits”指的是“microplastic”，即微塑料。原文中明确指出对微塑料的研究很少，这与题干的要求完全吻合。因此，正确答案是“microplastic”。

central_sentences.sentence: Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris, Rochman's group found little research on the effects of these tiny bits.
central_sentences.translation: 与研究大型垃圾的研究数量相比，Rochman的研究小组发现关于这些微小碎片影响的研究很少。

target_sen.target_sen: Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris, Rochman's group found little research on the effects of these tiny bits.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "populations"。题干中的 "individual animals" 和 "entire" 在原文中分别对应 "individual animal" 和 "whole"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案所在的句子是 "Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations."。这个句子明确指出，许多研究关注的是个体动物，而不是整个种群。因此，正确答案是 "populations"。

central_sentences.sentence: Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
central_sentences.translation: 许多研究关注塑料如何影响单个动物或其组织和细胞，而不是整个种群。

target_sen.target_sen: Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“used in the lab”在原文中对应“in the lab, scientists use”，“did not always reflect”对应“higher than what's really”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定文章中关键短语“higher concentrations of plastic”中的“concentrations”正是题干所需的词。题目要求填一个单词，而“concentrations”是名词，符合题目要求。

central_sentences.sentence: And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what's really in the ocean.
central_sentences.translation: 而且在实验室里，科学家们经常使用比实际海洋中更高浓度的塑料。

target_sen.target_sen: Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what's really in the ocean.
And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what's really in the ocean.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "impact" 对应原文中的 "affect"，"reduction in numbers" 对应 "deaths"，"of that species" 对应 "in one species"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案句中的 "predators" 是正确答案，因为它描述了一个物种数量减少对其捕食者的影响。

central_sentences.sentence: None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution — or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.
central_sentences.translation: 这些都无法告诉我们有多少鸟类、鱼类或海龟可能因塑料污染而死亡 - 或者一个物种的死亡如何影响该动物的捕食者，或者整个生态系统的其余部分。

target_sen.target_sen: None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution — or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.
Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution — or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.

questions.analysis: 答案是“disasters”，因为题干中的“possible impact of future”对应文章中的“how disasters...will affect the environment”。Rochman提到科学家通常不知道灾难对环境的影响，直到它们发生之后。因此，题干中的“possible impact of future”与文章中的“disasters”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Usually, scientists don't know exactly how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment until after they've happened.
central_sentences.translation: 通常情况下，科学家们在灾难发生之前并不确切知道诸如油轮意外泄漏全部货物并污染大片海域这样的灾难将如何影响环境。

target_sen.target_sen: 'We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions,' Rochman says.

syn_ary.synonym: We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions

target_sen.target_sen: 'We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions,' Rochman says.

groups.desc:
Question 40

Choose the correct answer, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What would be the best title for this passage?

options.text: Assessing the threat of marine debris
Marine debris: who is to blame?
A new solution to the problem of marine debris
Marine debris: the need for international action

questions.analysis: 这篇文章的主旨是评估海洋垃圾的威胁。文章从多个角度讨论了海洋垃圾的实际威胁和感知威胁之间的差异，并强调了需要更好地理解和评估这些威胁。因此，选项A“Assessing the threat of marine debris”最能概括文章的内容。

central_sentences.sentence: Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.
central_sentences.translation: Rochman和她的同事们着手确定有多少这些被认为的风险是真实存在的。
